001/**
002 * CommandWords class - stores all valid command words for
003 * the "Henriette's Feast" game and provides a way to check them.
004 * It is used by the parser to recognise which words are valid commands.
005 *
006 * @author Michael Kolling and David J. Barnes + D. Bureau + Raphaël Quillaud
007 * @version 2008.03.30 + 2019.09.25 + 2026.05.09
008 */
009public class CommandWords  {
010    // a constant array that will hold all valid command words
011    private static final String[] aValidCommands = {
012            "go", "quit", "help", "look", "eat", "back", "test", "take", "drop", "items", "charge", "fire"
013    };
014
015    /**
016     * Checks whether a given string is a valid command word.
017     *
018     * @param pString the word to test
019     * @return true if the given string is a valid command, false otherwise
020     */
021    public boolean isCommand( final String pString ) {
022        for ( int vI=0; vI<this.aValidCommands.length; vI++ ) {
023            if ( this.aValidCommands[vI].equals( pString ) )
024                return true;
025        } // for
026        // if we get here, the string was not found in the commands :
027        return false;
028    } // isCommand()
029
030    /**
031     * Returns all valid command words in a single string.
032     *
033     * @return a space-separated list of all commands
034     */
035    public String getCommandList() {
036        String vCommands = "";
037        for (int vI = 0; vI < this.aValidCommands.length; vI++) {
038            vCommands = vCommands + this.aValidCommands[vI] + " ";
039        }
040        return vCommands.trim();
041    }
042} // CommandWords
